questions.text: Which of the following is a proper fraction?
questions.explanation: A proper fraction has numerator smaller than denominator. In 3/5, 3 < 5, so it's a proper fraction.

questions.text: Two fractions with the same denominator are called like fractions.
questions.explanation: Like fractions have the same denominator. Unlike fractions have different denominators.
To convert a fraction to decimal, divide numerator by denominator: 3 ÷ 4 = 0.75

questions.text: A fraction with numerator 1 is called a ________ fraction.
questions.explanation: A unit fraction is a fraction with numerator 1. Examples: 1/2, 1/3, 1/4, etc.
